medical_history.chief_complaint: Congenital maxillary and mandibular alveolar soft tissue enlargement in the area of the premolars
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Congenital maxillary and mandibular alveolar soft tissue enlargement, abnormal trabeculation in the maxilla and mandible, congenitally missing maxillary and mandibular first and second premolars and mandibular canines

physical_examination.general_examination: Left maxillary and mandibular gingival enlargement, homolateral hypopigmentation and palsy
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Abnormal trabeculation in the maxilla and mandible, congenitally missing teeth, dysplastic woven bone with prominent reversal lines

laboratory_examinations.findings: Heterozygosity at a mosaic level (0.5–1.8%) for a single base substitution, c.1571G>A, in exon 10 of the coding sequence of PIK3CA which translates to a missense mutation, p.Arg524Lys, in the translated PIK3CA protein. The variant was seen at a low mosaic level of 2.0–3.7% in the blood.

radiographic_examinations.findings: Abnormal trabeculation essentially similar to SOD in the maxilla and mandible with congenitally missing maxillary and mandibular first and second premolars and mandibular canines.

Osseous abnormalities on the left side of the maxilla and mandible and very mild osseous expansion in the mandible. Abnormal maxillary bone in the area of the premolars exhibiting loss of lingual plate definition and lingual concavity when compared to the contralateral side.

other_tests.findings: Abnormal bone exhibiting prominent reversal lines was present and associated with fibrocollagenous tissue.

other_tests.procedure_name: Variant analysis of somatic overgrowth genes
other_tests.findings: Mutation analysis of somatic overgrowth related genes PIK3CA, AKT1, AKT2, AKT3, GNAQ, GNA11, MTOR, and PIK3R2 was undertaken using next generation sequencing (NGS) on the IonTorrent PGM platform.
